text.title: Stakeholder Communication Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding stakeholder-communication. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each session MUST commit to exactly one of the 5 modes; mode mixing within a session is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mode-mixing produces incoherent conversations: the stakeholder cannot tell whether to commit, explore, or critique.

rule.statement: Stakeholder map MUST place every stakeholder on the power/interest grid; the quadrant determines cadence + channel.
rule.rationale: Without quadrant placement, communication cadence is either over- or under-invested per stakeholder.

rule.statement: Validation mode MUST avoid Socratic probing — confirming understanding is incompatible with provoking re-evaluation.
rule.rationale: Socratic in Validation undoes the convergence the session was supposed to achieve.

rule.statement: Interview mode MUST use open questions (`active-listening` discipline). Closed yes/no is forbidden in Interview mode.
rule.rationale: Closed questions cap exploration; Interview's purpose is to expand the surface.

rule.statement: Validation mode MUST capture written confirmation (Slack note, email, doc edit). Verbal-only validation is forbidden because it doesn't persist.
rule.rationale: Memory diverges; written validation freezes the agreement.
